hero.question: Mexico City: James Duckworth vs Stefano Napolitano
hero.prob_take: At 24.5%, the crowd is giving Duckworth less than a 1-in-4 shot despite him being the higher-ranked player and a clay court specialist facing a qualifier in Mexico City. The 30-point drop in 24 hours smells like steam off sharp money, but you need to verify if Napolitano has a specific edge (injury intel, recent form, head-to-head) before chasing the contrarian play here.

daily_take.headline: Duckworth's collapse signals sharp money fleeing tennis futures
daily_take.deck: Duckworth tanked 30 points in 24 hours to 24.5%, a violent repricing that doesn't match the fundamentals of a Mexico City matchup against a lower-ranked player. This looks like liquidation or inside information bleeding through. Smart money shorts overextended Duckworth positions into weakness, or there's something about Napolitano's form you're not seeing in the headlines. Either way, at 24.5% Duckworth is now a contrarian buy if you've got conviction on the ranking differential.
